CodeEditorModel.Text:
'Code behind Page - Add local subroutines for the page.
Sub StaticMsgBox
' Get button choice using Enum number Dim buttons As S_MsgBox.eButtons buttons = CType(IndButton, S_MsgBox.eButtons)
' Get category choice using Enum number Dim category As S_MsgBox.eIcon category = CType(IndCategory, S_MsgBox.eIcon)
Dim iconRessource As String iconRessource = GetRessourceIcon(IndIcon)
' Call message box Call S_MsgBox.Show(MsgMessage, MsgCaption, category, buttons, iconRessource)
End Sub
''' <summary> ''' Using the "GetResourceString" function allows you to retrieve a text from a resource. ''' It returns the value corresponding to the current HMI language. ''' If the value is empty for the current language, the default language value (mandatory) is automatically returned. ''' </summary> Sub DynamicMsgBox
' Get ressource text from the HMI current language Dim txt As String txt = GetResourceString(MsgRessource)
' Call message box Call S_MsgBox.Show(txt, "Dynamic text", S_MsgBox.eButtons.OK) End Sub
''' <summary> ''' Retrieves the localized text from the resource and inserts a dynamic value into the message. ''' The placeholder "{name}" in the resource string is replaced by the user-defined value. ''' This allows dynamic content (such as a recipe name) to be displayed while keeping the text translatable. ''' ''' Example resource: ''' Recipe "{name}" has been successfully loaded from the database. ''' ''' Example usage: ''' Dim txt As String = GetResourceString("LE_Rcp_LoadConf_Recipe").Replace("{name}", txtUser) ''' </summary> Sub DynamicWithVariableMsgBox
' Get ressource text from the HMI current language Dim txt As String txt = GetResourceString("OBJ_Rcp_LoadDone_Recipe").Replace("{name}", MsgName)
' Call message box Call S_MsgBox.Show(txt, "Dynamic with variable text", S_MsgBox.eButtons.OK) End Sub
''' <summary> ''' In most cases, the result returned by the MessageBox is required to trigger a subsequent action. ''' The AddHandler statement registers a function that will handle the MessageBox response. ''' This function is executed once the user has responded to the dialog. ''' </summary> Sub ReturnMsgBox()
' Register the handler used to process the user's response. RemoveHandler S_MsgBox.Response, AddressOf _ReturnMsgBox ' Ensures that the handler is registered only once. AddHandler S_MsgBox.Response, AddressOf _ReturnMsgBox ' Defines the function to call after the user selects a button.
' The RemoveHandler statement is called first to avoid multiple registrations of the same handler. ' Without this step, each call to AddHandler would register another instance of the same handler, ' causing the Function To be executed multiple times when the event is raised. ' This guarantees that the MessageBox response is processed only once.
' Open the pop-up MessageBox. Call S_MsgBox.Show("Press a button !", "Return action", S_Msgbox.eIcon.Info, S_MsgBox.eButtons.YesNoCancel)
End Sub
''' <summary> ''' Handles the response returned by the MessageBox after the user has selected a button. ''' This subroutine can be used to execute a specific action depending on the selected result. ''' </summary> ''' <param name="button">Result returned by the MessageBox corresponding to the button selected by the user.</param> Sub _ReturnMsgBox(button As S_MsgBox.eResult)
' Unregister the handler after use. RemoveHandler S_MsgBox.Response, AddressOf _ReturnMsgBox ' Prevents the handler from remaining subscribed after the response has been processed.
' Get the button selected by the user. ' The value can then be used in an If statement or a Select Case block. MsgReturn = button.ToString()
' Example: display a new MessageBox showing the selected button. Dim txt As String = "The button pressed is: " & button.ToString() Call S_MsgBox.Show(txt, "Return action", S_Msgbox.eIcon.Success, S_MsgBox.eButtons.OK)
End Sub
#Region "Other"
''' <summary> ''' Return button enum ''' </summary> ''' <param name="index"></param> Function GetRessourceIcon(index As Integer)
Dim ressource As String
' Get button Select Case index Case 0 ressource = Nothing Case 1 ressource = "OBJ_FA_Home" Case 2 ressource = "OBJ_FA_Clock" Case 3 ressource = "OBJ_FA_Save" Case 4 ressource = "OBJ_FA_User" End Select
Return ressource End Function
#End Region
